jobs.title: AI Data Center Intern
jobs.description:
AI Data Center Internship
Position: Student Intern / Assistant – AI Data Center Construction & Operations
Location: DCX Goodyear 1 Data Center — Goodyear, Arizona
Company: DCX Goodyear 1, LLC (a DCX USA Company)
Reports To: Facility Manager, DCX Goodyear 1
Term:
Summer 2026 (May–August): Optional full-time internship opportunity (40 hours/week)
Position Overview
DCX Goodyear 1 invites students from Arizona State University’s Del E. Webb School of Construction or Ira A. Fulton School of Engineering at ASU to participate in an immersive learning experience at one of the nation’s most advanced AI-optimized data centers.
Students will gain practical exposure to how construction, power, mechanical, and digital systems converge to enable large-scale artificial intelligence and high-performance computing. Working directly with DCX’s Facility Manager and Operations Team, participants will observe live data-center operations, assist with documentation and project coordination, and learn how construction and facility management principles apply to mission-critical infrastructure.
This role is ideal for students exploring careers in engineering, construction management, MEP systems, or sustainable digital infrastructure.
Learning Objectives
Participants will:
Understand the design and operational requirements of AI data centers, including high-density power distribution, advanced cooling, and redundancy systems.
Learn how Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and Methods of Procedure (MOPs) govern reliability and uptime.
Observe commissioning and maintenance processes in an active critical-operations environment.
Gain familiarity with Building Management Systems (BMS), Electrical Power Monitoring Systems (EPMS), and Data Center Infrastructure Management (DCIM) platforms.
Explore how sustainability and efficiency (e.g., PUE, waterless cooling) are measured and achieved in modern facilities.
Develop professional communication, documentation, and safety-awareness skills essential to large-scale construction projects.
Responsibilities
Support the Facility Manager and Operations Technicians with site walkthroughs, system checks, and safety inspections.
Assist in updating drawings, as-built documentation, and maintenance logs.
Participate in project meetings and vendor coordination activities.
Collect and analyze operational data related to power, temperature, and environmental conditions.
Prepare a final reflection or presentation summarizing key learning outcomes to DCX leadership and, if applicable, ASU faculty.
Qualifications
Required
Enrollment as a junior, senior, or graduate student in the Del E. Webb School of Construction or Ira A. Fulton School of Engineering at ASU.
Interest in engineering, construction management, mechanical/electrical systems, or digital infrastructure.
Ability to be on-site at DCX Goodyear 1 one day per week during the academic term (or full-time during summer, if applicable).
Strong written, verbal, and organizational skills.
Professional attitude and adherence to safety and security protocols.
Preferred
Active involvement in one or more construction-related student organizations (e.g., ASU AGC, DBIA Student Chapter, MCAA, Women in Construction).
Coursework or experience in MEP coordination, energy systems, or project management.
Familiarity with AutoCAD, Revit, Bluebeam, or Power BI.
Demonstrated interest in AI, high-density computing, or sustainable facility design.
Program Details
Summer 2026 Option: Full-time internship (≈ 40 hours/week)
Compensation: Paid (competitive hourly rate)
Location: DCX Goodyear 1 Data Center, Goodyear, Arizona
Deliverable: End-of-term report or presentation
Educational Value
This experience connects ASU construction students with the mission-critical infrastructure industry, a rapidly growing sector supporting artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and sustainable energy systems. Students will leave with practical understanding of how large-scale data facilities are built, maintained, and optimized, strengthening their readiness for advanced roles in construction and operations.
About DCX USA
DCX USA designs, builds, and operates next-generation, AI-optimized data centers that deliver unmatched reliability, energy efficiency, and scalability. DCX Goodyear 1 is the company’s flagship facility in Arizona, demonstrating the integration of intelligent power systems, advanced cooling, and sustainable construction practices for AI and high-performance computing.
Learn more at www.dcx.us.
